title: Material Trades Made
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS trades FROM MaterialTrade WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate
detailQuery: SELECT (Paid_Category || ' → ' || Received_Category) AS label, COUNT(*) AS value FROM MaterialTrade WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate GROUP BY Paid_Category, Received_Category ORDER BY value DESC LIMIT 20

details.text: {trades} material trade transaction(s) at material traders during the report period. Material trading is how you solve inventory gaps without grinding for specific items — the flow from cheaper to rarer grades is a key part of engineering pipeline optimisation for serious builders.
details.help: MaterialTrade Paid_Category and Received_Category reflect the material categories exchanged. trades drives the title as the total transaction count.

tagLines: Material trader transaction log compiled. Material grade conversion activity noted in the crafting record.
Trading down to trade up. The inefficiency is the cost of not wanting to grind the specific node.
Material traders exist because the galaxy understands that you don't always have what you need in the grade you need it.
